summary: SEC-001 Secrets Management Architecture - All tests passed (46/46). Backend starts correctly with secrets manager initialized. Auth login works. Exely and HotelRunner connection endpoints don't leak credentials. Test connection endpoints work without crashing. Health endpoint returns healthy status.
critical_code_review_comments: Secrets manager architecture is well-designed with proper separation of concerns
Legacy fallback mechanism allows gradual migration without breaking existing connections
Audit logging is properly implemented with TTL indexes for automatic cleanup
Credential masking utility correctly handles both long and short values
Connection endpoints properly exclude sensitive fields (password, username, token, credentials_ref)
context_for_next_testing_agent: SEC-001 secrets management is fully implemented and tested. 35 unit tests cover config validation, naming conventions, local/AWS/Vault providers, SecretsManager CRUD, credential masking, and audit logging. 11 API integration tests verify auth login, connection endpoints don't leak credentials, test endpoints don't crash, and health endpoint works. All tests pass. The local_dev backend uses MongoDB with AES-256-GCM encryption. AWS Secrets Manager is mocked in unit tests only.

categories: Exely connection no credential leak (2 tests)
HotelRunner connection no credential leak (2 tests)
No plaintext secrets in responses (2 tests)
